regions.name: Lima
regions.stay_type: boutique hotel in Miraflores or Barranco

activities.category: Barranco arts district and coastal walk
Lima food scene — tasting menu at a renowned Peruvian restaurant
Larco Museum pre-Columbian collection

regions.name: Sacred Valley
regions.stay_type: hacienda or eco-lodge with mountain views

activities.category: Pisac market and Inca terrace complex
Salt flats at Maras and Moray circular terraces
Local weaving community visit with textile history

regions.name: Machu Picchu
regions.stay_type: lodge in Aguas Calientes with early-entry access

activities.category: Dawn entry to Machu Picchu citadel
Huayna Picchu or Sun Gate hike (booked months ahead)
Hot springs at Aguas Calientes evening wind-down

regions.name: Cusco
regions.stay_type: colonial-convent boutique in San Blas or the Plaza

activities.category: Sacsayhuamán fortress and local market circuit
Cooking class focused on Andean ingredients

common_mistakes: Flying directly from sea level to Cusco (3,400m) without an acclimatization day — altitude sickness ruins more trips here than anything else; start with the Sacred Valley
Booking Huayna Picchu or Machu Picchu Mountain permits less than 3 months ahead — they sell out entirely
Visiting in the wet season (November–April) without understanding that trails close and the Inca Trail is shut entirely in February
Spending only one day at Machu Picchu — two days lets you see it in different light and explore without rushing

activities.category: Lima food scene quick introduction
Macaw clay lick at dawn — one of the world's great wildlife spectacles
Jungle trail ecology walk with indigenous naturalist
Oxbow lake at night — caiman and nocturnal wildlife
Giant river otter territory at Sandoval Lake

regions.name: Sacred Valley & Machu Picchu
regions.stay_type: hacienda in the valley, lodge in Aguas Calientes

activities.category: Sacred Valley market and ruin circuit
Machu Picchu dawn entry with private guide
Andean village homestay or community meal

common_mistakes: Not bringing professional rain gear to the Amazon — even in dry season afternoon storms are common and the lodge will be 45 minutes by boat from the nearest shelter
Combining the Amazon with Cusco altitude in the same week without planning the acclimatization sequence carefully
Visiting Tambopata during the Brazilian nut harvest season when the lodges prioritize sustainability-forest work over tourism activities

regions.name: Cusco
regions.stay_type: colonial-convent boutique hotel in San Blas or near the Plaza de Armas

activities.category: Sacsayhuamán fortress at sunrise — acclimatization walk at Inca pace
Cusco gastronomy circuit — ceviche de sierra, chupe, novo-Andean tasting menu
San Blas artists' quarter and artisan market circuit

regions.name: Sacred Valley (Valle Sagrado)
regions.stay_type: hacienda or eco-lodge with Andean mountain views

activities.category: Pisac Inca terrace complex and Sunday market
Salt flats at Maras and Moray circular agricultural terraces
Sound healing or plant medicine ceremony with a curandera (optional)
Ollantaytambo fortress — last Inca stronghold and gateway to Machu Picchu

regions.name: Machu Picchu
regions.stay_type: lodge in Aguas Calientes with early-entry ticket access

activities.category: Dawn entry to Machu Picchu citadel before the tour groups arrive
Huayna Picchu or Sun Gate hike — book permit months in advance
Hot springs at Aguas Calientes — evening wind-down after the climb

common_mistakes: Flying directly from sea level to Cusco (3,400 m) — altitude sickness ruins more Peru trips than anything else; start in the Sacred Valley to acclimatize first
Booking Huayna Picchu or Machu Picchu Mountain permits less than 3 months ahead — they sell out completely
Underestimating the Sacred Valley as 'just the way to Machu Picchu' — the valley itself deserves 3–4 nights; most guests wish they had stayed longer
Visiting in February when the Inca Trail is fully closed for maintenance and heavy rains affect most highland trails

regions.name: Lima (transit)
regions.stay_type: hotel in Miraflores — convenient for the domestic connection

activities.category: Lima food scene quick introduction — ceviche and pisco sour in Barranco

regions.name: Tarapoto (Alto Mayo & San Martín region)
regions.stay_type: riverside jungle lodge or boutique eco-stay on the city's edge

activities.category: Plant medicine retreat or guided ceremony in the high jungle (optional — vetted operator required)
Huallaga River waterfall circuit — Catarata Ahuashiyacu and Shilcayo
Cacao farm visit and traditional chocolate preparation with local growers
Dawn birdwatching in the Alto Mayo AICA corridor — one of Peru's least-visited birding zones

regions.name: Sacred Valley & Machu Picchu
regions.stay_type: hacienda in the valley, lodge in Aguas Calientes

activities.category: Sacred Valley market and Inca ruin circuit at Pisac and Ollantaytambo
Machu Picchu dawn entry with private guide
Andean community meal or textile workshop in a local village

common_mistakes: Booking a plant medicine ceremony without vetting the operator thoroughly — research reputation and safety protocols well in advance
Combining Tarapoto jungle heat with Cusco altitude in the same week without planning the acclimatization sequence carefully
Treating Tarapoto as a quick add-on — it requires its own domestic flight from Lima and rewards at least 3–4 nights
Skipping Tarapoto's bird corridors if wildlife is a priority — the Alto Mayo region is one of Peru's best-kept birding secrets
